name: Cellpose (Generalist Cell Segmentation)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD-3-Clause); Carsen Stringer / Howard Hughes Medical Institute

auth.notes: Local library; pretrained models download automatically

performance.notes: PyTorch GPU-accelerated; tile-based processing for large images

agent_readiness.notes: Generalist cell segmentation for microscopy images. from cellpose import models. model = models.Cellpose(gpu=True, model_type='cyto3'). masks, flows, styles, diams = model.eval(imgs, diameter=30, channels=[0,0]). masks: labeled segmentation mask. model.train(imgs, masks, channels, save_path='my_model'). CLI: python -m cellpose --image_path img.tif --pretrained_model cyto3. Models: cyto, cyto2, cyto3, nuclei, tissuenet. Batch: model.eval(imgs_list). diameter=None for auto-detection. napari-cellpose for GUI.

gotchas: GPU strongly recommended — CPU is slow for large images
diameter parameter is critical — wrong value causes poor segmentation
channels=[cytoplasm, nucleus] convention must be followed
3D segmentation requires diameter calibration in z-direction
best_when: Cell segmentation in fluorescence microscopy images
Nucleus segmentation (nuclei model) or cytoplasm (cyto3 model)
When generalist pre-trained model eliminates manual threshold tuning
Custom cell type fine-tuning from a few examples
avoid_when: H&E histology segmentation (use StarDist or QuPath)
When instance segmentation is needed beyond cells (use Detectron2)
Low GPU resources for large image datasets
summary: Cellpose provides generalist cell segmentation for microscopy. models.Cellpose(gpu=True, model_type='cyto3').eval(imgs, diameter=30, channels=[0,0]). Returns labeled mask, flows. Train on custom data. Models: cyto3, nuclei, tissuenet. BSD-3 by Carsen Stringer/HHMI. GPU recommended. diameter parameter critical for quality. Best for fluorescence microscopy cell/nucleus segmentation without manual thresholds.
